commonProblems.h2: Aston Martin Lagonda Engine Problems - What It Costs to Fix & When Replacement Makes Sense
commonProblems.h3: The Aston Martin Lagonda's 5.3L V8 is a complex, low-volume engine where electrical gremlins often overshadow mechanical issues. Timing chain tensioner wear, oil leaks and fuel injection sensor failures dominate replacement enquiries. Every rebuilt engine supplied through EngineMarket includes upgraded internal components designed to address the original failure point - plus a minimum 12‑month unlimited mileage warranty.

problems.h4: Timing Chain Tensioner Failure - Why Aston Martin Lagonda V8 Engines Rattle on Startup
problems.affectedModels: Aston Martin Lagonda Series 2, 3, 4 (1976-1990, V/580 5.3L V8)
problems.rootCause: Original hydraulic tensioners lose pressure with age, allowing chain slack and cam timing drift.

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Replace hydraulic tensioner, inspect chain guides, reset timing
repairOptions.longevity: Viable if caught early before chain stretch; 20,000-40,000 mile extension

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Full timing chain kit, tensioners, guides, seals; engine partially stripped
repairOptions.longevity: Recommended for high-mileage engines; addresses root cause comprehensively

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Supply & fit rebuilt V/580 with upgraded components, bench tested, 12-month warranty
repairOptions.longevity: Best value if engine has other wear; eliminates all timing-related risks permanently

problems.recommendation: On a Lagonda worth £40,000-£120,000, preventative tensioner replacement at 60k miles is cost-effective. If rattling is present, budget for full chain kit or consider rebuilt engine if other wear items are due.
problems.cta: Compare rebuilt V/580 engine prices for your Aston Martin Lagonda

problems.h4: Oil Leak Repair - Valve Covers & Rear Main Seal on Aston Martin Lagonda V8
problems.affectedModels: Aston Martin Lagonda Series 2, 3, 4 (1976-1990, V/580 5.3L V8)
problems.rootCause: Age-hardened rubber gaskets and seals on the 5.3L V8 allow oil to seep from valve covers and rear crank seal.

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Replace valve cover gaskets, sump seal, inspect rear main seal access
repairOptions.longevity: Suitable for early-stage seepage; 2-4 year typical lifespan

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Remove gearbox to access rear main seal, replace all external seals
repairOptions.longevity: Recommended for persistent leaks; addresses most common leak points

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Supply & fit rebuilt V/580 with upgraded seals throughout, bench tested, 12-month warranty
repairOptions.longevity: Best if engine has high mileage or other wear; eliminates all leak risks

problems.recommendation: For minor seepage, gasket renewal is cost-effective. If oil loss is significant or rear main seal is leaking, weigh the cost of gearbox removal against a rebuilt engine with fresh seals throughout.
problems.cta: Get quotes for Aston Martin Lagonda V/580 oil leak repair

problems.h4: Fuel Injection Sensor Failure - Series 3/4 Aston Martin Lagonda Bosch L-Jetronic Issues
problems.affectedModels: Aston Martin Lagonda Series 3, 4 (1986-1990, V/580 fuel injection)
problems.rootCause: Ageing wiring harness insulation, corroded connectors and failed sensors disrupt Bosch L-Jetronic ECU signals.

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Replace failed MAF, TPS or coolant temp sensor; basic wiring repair
repairOptions.longevity: Suitable for isolated sensor failure; may not resolve harness-wide degradation

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Full wiring harness re-sleeving, connector renewal, ECU capacitor replacement
repairOptions.longevity: Recommended for multiple electrical faults; restores factory-spec reliability

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Supply & fit rebuilt fuel-injected V/580 with refurbished harness, bench tested, 12-month warranty
repairOptions.longevity: Best for cars with combined mechanical and electrical wear; eliminates all FI risks

problems.recommendation: For Series 3/4 Lagondas with rough running, start with sensor diagnostics. If multiple codes appear or harness is brittle, harness refurbishment is often more cost-effective than chasing individual faults.
problems.cta: Compare rebuilt Series 3 fuel injection engine prices for your Aston Martin Lagonda

problems.h4: Cooling System Inadequacy - Overheating in Traffic on Aston Martin Lagonda V8
problems.affectedModels: Aston Martin Lagonda Series 2, 3, 4 (1976-1990, V/580 5.3L V8)
problems.typicalFailureMileage: Any mileage; design limitation exacerbated by age
problems.rootCause: Original radiator and fan capacity insufficient for modern traffic conditions; aged hoses and water pumps compound the issue.

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Fit upgraded electric fan, thermostat check, coolant flush
repairOptions.longevity: Suitable for mild overheating; improves low-speed cooling marginally

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Fit aluminium radiator, silicone hoses, upgraded water pump
repairOptions.longevity: Recommended for regular urban use; addresses core cooling capacity

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Supply & fit rebuilt V/580 with upgraded cooling components, bench tested, 12-month warranty
repairOptions.longevity: Best if overheating has caused head gasket or warping damage

problems.recommendation: Preventative cooling upgrades are wise for any Lagonda used in traffic. If overheating has occurred, inspect for head gasket damage before deciding between cooling system repair or full engine replacement.
problems.cta: Get quotes for Aston Martin Lagonda cooling system upgrade or engine replacement

problems.h4: Weber Carburettor Tuning Issues - Cold Starting & Running Problems on Series 2 Lagonda
problems.affectedModels: Aston Martin Lagonda Series 2 (1976-1985, V/580 Weber 48 IDA)
problems.typicalFailureMileage: Any; tuning sensitivity increases with wear
problems.rootCause: Weber 48 IDA 4-barrel carburettors require precise synchronization and jetting; wear in throttle shafts and linkages disrupts fuel mixture.

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Clean, re-jet, synchronize Weber 48 IDA; replace worn linkages
repairOptions.longevity: Suitable for mild running issues; requires specialist carburettor knowledge

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Full strip, bushings, new throttle shafts, calibration on flow bench
repairOptions.longevity: Recommended for persistent cold-start or idle problems; restores factory spec

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Supply & fit rebuilt V/580 with serviced Weber carbs, bench tested, 12-month warranty
repairOptions.longevity: Best if engine has high mileage or combined mechanical issues

problems.recommendation: For Series 2 Lagondas with running issues, start with a specialist carburettor service. If throttle shaft wear is present, budget for full rebuild or consider fuel injection conversion (value-impacting).
problems.cta: Compare rebuilt Series 2 Weber engine prices for your Aston Martin Lagonda

finalCta.h4: Don't Let Engine Failure Write Off Your Aston Martin Lagonda
finalCta.paragraph: The Aston Martin Lagonda's ultra-low production numbers mean specialist knowledge is non-negotiable for engine work. EngineMarket connects you with UK rebuilders who understand V/580 nuances, from Weber calibration to Bosch L-Jetronic diagnostics. Every rebuilt engine includes upgraded components addressing known failure points, bench testing, and a minimum 12‑month unlimited mileage warranty. Compare quotes today and preserve your Lagonda's provenance without the main dealer premium.
finalCta.buttonText: Compare Aston Martin Lagonda Engine Replacement Prices Now →
finalCta.disclaimer: All prices are indicative UK market ranges based on historical quote data and repair requests from our network of engine specialists. Dealer pricing is estimated based on published main dealer labour rates and genuine OEM parts pricing. Specialist pricing reflects actual quote data from EngineMarket's UK network. Actual costs vary depending on damage severity, vehicle condition, variant, and workshop location within the UK. Use our free comparison tool to get tailored quotes for your specific Aston Martin Lagonda and engine code. Every engine supplied through EngineMarket carries a minimum 12‑month unlimited mileage warranty - always confirm exact terms with your chosen supplier.

engineSizes.h2: Aston Martin Lagonda Engines by Size - Find the Right Displacement for Your Variant
engineSizes.intro: Every displacement fitted to the Aston Martin Lagonda is listed below, with compatible engine codes, Series years and common failure notes to help you identify the right replacement.

groups.title: Aston Martin Lagonda Petrol Engine Sizes

items.title: Aston Martin Lagonda 5.3L Petrol Engines
items.description: The 5.3-litre V8 (5,340cc) powered every production Aston Martin Lagonda from Series 1 (1974) through Series 4 (1990), with Weber carburettors on Series 2 and Bosch L-Jetronic fuel injection introduced for Series 3/4. This undersquare design (96mm bore × 85mm stroke) prioritises low-end torque for the Lagonda's grand touring character.
commonFailurePoints: oil leaks from valve covers/rear main seal
Bosch L-Jetronic sensor/harness degradation on Series 3/4
items.cta: Compare Aston Martin Lagonda 5.3L petrol engine prices

engineYears.h2: Aston Martin Lagonda Engines by Year - What Was Fitted & When
engineYears.intro: This section covers all major engine changes across the Aston Martin Lagonda's production timeline (1974-1990), organised so owners can quickly identify their V/580 engine and compare rebuilt prices from UK specialists.

years.description: The rarest Lagonda variant, with only 7 cars built using early 5.3L V8 development engines.
keyChanges: First Lagonda prototype series, 4-door saloon with V8 power
Engine numbers not yet standardised to V/580 prefix format
Shared architecture with contemporary Aston Martin V8 models
knownFor: Extreme rarity (7 built) driving premium parts pricing
Engine numbers require Heritage Trust verification for provenance
Mechanicals largely shared with AM V8, aiding parts sourcing
Matching-numbers preservation critical for collector value
years.cta: Get quotes for 1974 Aston Martin Lagonda replacement engines

years.description: The definitive Lagonda, with razor-edge styling and Weber-carburetted 5.3L V8 powering 563 cars.
keyChanges: William Towns' iconic angular design launched
Digital dashboard and touch-sensitive switches introduced (notorious for electrical issues)
popularModels: Aston Martin Lagonda Series 2 Digital Dash
knownFor: Weber 48 IDA 4-barrel carburettor requiring specialist tuning
Timing chain tensioner wear causing startup rattle on high-mileage units
Digital dash electronics failures often overshadowing mechanical issues
Highest replacement enquiry volume due to 563-unit production run

faq.h2: Aston Martin Lagonda Engine Replacement - Frequently Asked Questions

items.question: How much does an Aston Martin Lagonda engine rebuild cost?
items.answer: Lagonda V8 rebuild costs range from £18,000 for Series 2 Weber units to £35,000+ for Series 3/4 fuel injection or Tickford specifications, depending on core condition and matching-numbers requirements.
keyPoints: Series 2 Weber rebuilds typically £18,000-£25,000 supply only
Series 3/4 fuel injection adds £7,000-£10,000 for ECU/harness work
Tickford-spec rebuilds command £30,000-£45,000 due to rarity
Matching-numbers preservation adds premium but protects collector value
items.cta: Compare rebuilt Aston Martin Lagonda engine prices today

items.question: Is the Aston Martin Lagonda V/580 engine reliable?
items.answer: The 5.3L V8 is robust mechanically but suffers from age-related electrical and sealing issues; proactive maintenance of timing components and cooling system is essential for longevity.
keyPoints: Timing chain tensioner wear is the most documented mechanical failure point
Oil leaks from valve covers and rear main seal are common wear items
Series 3/4 fuel injection electronics require specialist diagnostics
Regular servicing with correct 20W-50 high-ZDDP oil extends life significantly
items.cta: Get quotes for Aston Martin Lagonda V/580 preventative maintenance

items.question: Can I replace my Lagonda engine with a non-matching V/580 unit?
items.answer: Physically yes, all 5.3L V8s 1976-1990 interchange, but non-matching numbers reduce collector value by 20-30% on these ultra-rare 645-unit cars.
keyPoints: All V/580 engines share identical block architecture and mounts
Series 3/4 fuel injection requires full harness and ECU swap
DVLA and Heritage Trust verification essential for provenance
Exchange engines save downtime but sacrifice matching-numbers premium
items.cta: Compare matching-numbers Aston Martin Lagonda engine rebuild options

items.question: What causes timing chain rattle on Aston Martin Lagonda V8 engines?
items.answer: Ageing hydraulic tensioners lose pressure, allowing chain slack; if ignored, this can lead to cam timing drift, valve damage and catastrophic engine failure.
keyPoints: Tensioner failure typically occurs 60,000-100,000 miles or 30+ years age
Rattle on cold startup is the classic early warning symptom
Preventative replacement costs £1,800-£3,000 vs £18k+ for full rebuild
Upgraded tensioner kits available from Lagonda specialists
items.cta: Get quotes for Aston Martin Lagonda timing chain replacement

items.question: Are Weber carburettors or fuel injection better for Lagonda reliability?
items.answer: Weber carbs are mechanically simpler and easier to tune, while fuel injection offers better driveability but adds electronic complexity and higher repair costs on aged systems.
keyPoints: Weber 48 IDA requires specialist calibration but fewer failure points
Bosch L-Jetronic sensors and harness degrade with age, causing rough running
Conversion between systems is possible but value-impacting and costly (£8k-£12k)
Choose based on intended use: Weber for simplicity, FI for modern driveability
items.cta: Compare Aston Martin Lagonda Weber vs fuel injection engine prices

items.question: How long does an Aston Martin Lagonda engine rebuild take?
items.answer: Typical lead time is 6-10 months due to limited specialist machine shop capacity and parts sourcing for this ultra-low-volume model.
keyPoints: Core inspection and parts sourcing adds 2-3 months upfront
Precision machining and assembly requires 3-4 months
Weber calibration or FI ECU refurbishment adds specialist time
Exchange engine schemes can reduce downtime to 2-4 weeks
items.cta: Check Aston Martin Lagonda engine rebuild lead times and availability

items.question: Do I need a specialist to rebuild my Lagonda V/580 engine?
items.answer: Absolutely - the V/580's low production numbers, matching-numbers requirements and Series-specific fuel systems demand Lagonda-experienced rebuilders.
keyPoints: Only a handful of UK workshops have Lagonda V8 rebuilding expertise
Matching-numbers restamping requires legal declaration and Heritage documentation
Weber calibration and Bosch L-Jetronic diagnostics need specialist equipment
Generalist rebuilders risk incorrect clearances or calibration errors
items.cta: Find vetted Aston Martin Lagonda engine specialists in our network

items.question: What warranty comes with a rebuilt Aston Martin Lagonda engine?
items.answer: Rebuilt engines supplied through EngineMarket carry a minimum 12-month unlimited mileage warranty on parts and labour, with some specialists offering 24-month coverage.
keyPoints: Warranty covers machining, components and assembly workmanship
Fuel injection ECUs and Weber carbs may have separate coverage terms
Supply & fit installations include post-fit commissioning warranty
Always confirm exact terms with your chosen specialist before commissioning
items.cta: Compare warranty terms on Aston Martin Lagonda rebuilt engines

items.question: Can I upgrade my Lagonda engine during rebuild for more power or reliability?
items.answer: Yes - common upgrades include forged pistons, upgraded timing kits, aluminium radiators and ECU remapping, but must balance performance gains against matching-numbers value impact.
keyPoints: Forged pistons and upgraded cams can raise output to 330-350hp
Aluminium radiators and electric fans address original cooling limitations
Any modifications should be documented and reversible for provenance
Performance upgrades may affect insurance and collector classification
items.cta: Discuss Aston Martin Lagonda engine upgrade options with a specialist

items.question: Is it worth replacing the engine on an Aston Martin Lagonda?
items.answer: Given Lagonda values of £40,000-£120,000+, a £18,000-£35,000 rebuilt engine is often cost-effective versus parting out, especially when preserving matching-numbers provenance.
keyPoints: Matching-numbers cars command 20-30% premium in collector market
Rebuild cost is typically 30-50% of a good-condition Lagonda's value
Exchange engines reduce downtime but sacrifice provenance premium
EngineMarket's comparison tool helps find best-value specialist quotes
items.cta: Get tailored quotes for your Aston Martin Lagonda engine replacement
